title: RenoDX manifest
description: Overrides + catalogue manifest for RenoDX HDR installs. Carries NO hashes/sizes: add-ons are fetched live from upstream (clshortfuse.github.io and engine-generic repos), so this document only maps games to their slug + match rules + per-game overrides, plus the global ReShade sources, engine-generic fallbacks, and shared defaults. Schema version 3 introduces a top-level `defaults` block so the per-title `risk`/`min_app_version`/`channel`/`compatibility` boilerplate is emitted only when it deviates from the defaults (the app merges `defaults` with each title). The Rust validator in renderpilot-orchestration (renodx::parse_manifest) is the canonical gatekeeper; this schema mirrors it for editor tooling and CI.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp, date-only granularity (T00:00:00Z). Honors SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH for reproducible builds.

generics.description: Engine-detected fallbacks, tried when no per-game title matches.

defaults.description: Shared title defaults. The app applies these to every title that omits the corresponding field; the generator only emits a per-title field when it differs from these.

titles.description: The single catalogue of every game RenoDX knows. Each entry is matched by its rules and optionally routed by `category` (external / native-HDR / blacklist). Omitted category means installable.

slug.description: RenoDX add-on slug = the upstream src/games folder name (e.g. cp2077, jedisurvivor, _univ). The add-on file is renodx-<slug>.addon64/32.

reshade.description: Global add-on-enabled ReShade host sources. Stable is the manifest-current versioned reshade.me add-on installer; nightly is crosire CI via nightly.link.

url.description: Versioned reshade.me ReShade_Setup_<version>_Addon.exe URL. Update this manifest field when stable advances.

generic.description: An engine-generic add-on. `slug` is the canonical local add-on identity; optional explicit url64/url32 override the download host for generics published outside clshortfuse.github.io (e.g. Unity at NotVoosh/renodx-unity). Entries without a slug must provide both explicit URLs.

category.description: How a matched game is routed. Omitted = installable (a normal RenoDX install); the other kinds categorize the game and carry their payload.

oneOf.description: Add-on distributed off-GitHub (Discord/Nexus): the app links out + offers a manual file install.

tier.description: Specificity; higher wins. Conventionally id 100, fingerprint 90, exe-name 70, engine 40, generic 10.

compatibility.description: Compatibility constraints. When omitted, the app treats a title as {required_arch: title.arch, required_api: [], conflicts: []}. required_arch is therefore never emitted (it always equals arch); this object only appears when required_api or conflicts are non-empty.

defaults.description: Title defaults applied by the app when a title omits the field.

channel.description: Default release channel. The generator derives stable for working titles and beta otherwise, then only emits a per-title channel when it differs from this default.

title.description: A catalogue entry — a game RenoDX knows, matched by its rules and routed by `category` (default installable). risk / min_app_version / channel / compatibility are omitted when they equal the top-level defaults (the app merges).

status.description: Upstream wiki test-map status: working = verified, construction = WIP/experimental, unknown = untested.

download_url.description: Direct download URL for add-ons hosted outside the official clshortfuse snapshot (e.g. a third-party github.io). The app fetches this instead of the snapshot asset.

allOf.description: A categorized title routes away from direct install; download_url is only for installable off-snapshot add-ons.
